message:
refactor(channel): remove unnecessary mut qualifiers from oneshot receivers

The oneshot and tracked_oneshot receiver types no longer require
mutable access for recv operations (the internal state is behind
interior mutability). This removes 'mut' from receiver bindings
across the codebase:

- src/channel/oneshot.rs: 4 test functions (value_is_moved_not_cloned,
  recv_cancel_after_pending_clears_registered_waker,
  recv_future_drop_clears_stale_waker,
  recv_repoll_same_waker_keeps_waiter_identity)
- src/channel/session.rs: 3 test functions (tracked_oneshot_send_recv,
  tracked_oneshot_abort, tracked_oneshot_convenience_send)
- src/gen_server.rs: 2 call sites in GenServerHandle::call and
  GenServerRef::call where the reply receiver is created
- tests/e2e_messaging.rs: e2e_oneshot_rendezvous test
- tests/runtime_e2e.rs: e2e_obligation_tracked_oneshot_abort test

Eliminates compiler warnings and reflects the actual API contract.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
